Target the Shenandoah River main article or regional hydrology/encyclopedic pages (lead, course section, or infobox) that summarize the river’s entire course from its forks to its confluence with the Potomac — specifically looking for language that ties the river to named places (Harpers Ferry/Potomac) and explicitly names the state(s) it traverses.

Target geographic or town-level pages (Harpers Ferry, Potomac confluence descriptions), course subsections, or map captions that state where the Shenandoah meets the Potomac and identify the state at that confluence — thereby establishing that the Shenandoah’s downstream terminus lies at a place in West Virginia while linking back to Virginia headwaters.
